text: Entering this place, I feel a sense of curiosity mixed with unease. The dim lighting, sterile glass, and scattered materials on the floor evoke a liminal and somewhat uncanny atmosphere. Transitioning into the brightly colored, text-adorned exhibit, I am greeted by the promise of research and development, and my apprehension is replaced by intrigue. The space encourages contemplation about our relationship with intelligent machines and technology, inviting me to reflect on how we connect, innovate, and coexist in a rapidly changing world. I feel both observed and observing, present at the threshold of the artificial and the human.
